Parco Ciani – Lugano's Most Beautiful City Park

Just a 25-minute walk from the city center or easily accessible by bus line 2, Parco Ciani is considered one of the most beautiful parks in Switzerland. Located directly on the lakeshore, it uniquely combines botany, art, and water views.

🌿 What to expect:
Lovingly tended flowerbeds, sculptures, and palm trees
A picturesque lakeside promenade with benches
Expansive lawns, shady avenues, and venerable trees
Photo opportunities with views of Lake Lugano and the surrounding mountains

📸 Tip: The area around the neoclassical Villa Ciani building and the lakeshore with its colorful flowerbeds is one of the most popular photo spots in Lugano – ideal for a relaxing morning or afternoon stroll.

Just steps from the bustling center of Lugano, Parco Ciani extends, a destination for countless tourists who flock there to admire what is considered one of the most beautiful parks in Switzerland.

Overlooking the lake, with its 63,000 square meters of surface area, it is the city's green lung. Parco Ciani, thanks to its enviable location just steps from the center of Lugano, is a place where both Lugano residents and tourists relax, strolling in the shade of centuries-old trees. The paths wind through beautiful flowerbeds, adorned with statues and fountains, and wind through manicured English lawns. The park is divided into two areas. The first, near Villa Ciani, can be reached from the main entrance, located at the end of the lakeside promenade. Here, the park features both Italian and English garden features: large flowerbeds alternate with patches of lush green grass and trees and shrubs from around the world. The second area extends from the dock to the Cassarate River and has a more "wild" and decidedly indigenous feel: oaks, lime trees, plane trees, and maples, all typical of Ticino's forests. Within this wooded area is a children's area, complete with play equipment and suitable for all ages. Ciani Park also houses several buildings: Villa Ciani, the Congress Center, the dock, the Cantonal Museum of Natural History, and the Cantonal Library.

A magnificent place, a well-located park opposite the lake in the city of Lugano, well maintained, it offers a magnificent view of the city center and has seating for the comfort of visitors as well as many other activities for children and adults. If you are passing through Lugano, consider visiting it, I recommend it 100%.

Ciani Park in Lugano on Lake Lugano is open daily. Admission is free.

Founded in the 19th century by the Ciani family, it was acquired by the Lugano municipality in 1912 and became a public promenade along Lake Lugano.

It is the largest park in Lugano and one of the most beautiful in Switzerland. It is frequently visited by Lugano residents and tourists, for whom a photo at the metal gate above the lake is a tradition. The park, covering a very large area of ​​63,000 m², is home to a diverse range of subtropical and Mediterranean flora, such as palms, camellias, magnolias, azaleas, roses, and much more. The park is intersected by numerous paths, along which hundreds of meters of flowerbeds with colorful and diverse flowers and shrubs have been planted. The park also features statues, fountains, a playground, and other attractions. Undoubtedly, the park is an oasis of peace and relaxation amidst greenery overlooking the lake, well worth a visit.

Lugano, Parco Ciani, Ciani Park is the green gem of Lugano in the city center, in the historic center, on an area of ​​63,000 square meters. The park is located in a perfect geographical position on the shores of the lake, with a temperate climate ideal for lush subtropical vegetation. One of the main attractions of the park is the seasonal flowering vegetation, perfectly integrated into the lake and the surrounding panorama. The tree species in the park are native to the Ticino forests: oak, linden, maple, plane tree.

Park Ciani is an absolute gem in the heart of Lugano! This beautifully maintained park offers a peaceful escape with its lush greenery, stunning views of Lake Lugano, and well-kept walking paths. It’s the perfect place to relax, have a picnic, or enjoy a leisurely stroll. The flowerbeds and trees are meticulously cared for, creating a serene atmosphere that makes you feel at peace. It’s also family-friendly, with plenty of space for children to play. Whether you’re visiting for the first time or returning, Park Ciani never fails to impress. Highly recommend for anyone looking to enjoy nature in Lugano!

Review of Parco Ciani – Lugano
Parco Ciani is undoubtedly one of the most beautiful places I've ever visited. I visited in May last year and was enchanted: meticulously maintained, immaculately clean, and immersed in a magical atmosphere amidst the lake, flower-filled meadows, and majestic centuries-old trees.
Every corner exudes peace and beauty, ideal for a relaxing stroll or simply to enjoy nature in complete tranquility.
I can't wait to return again this May, confident of finding the same care and charm that make this park truly unique.
A place I recommend to anyone visiting Lugano: unmissable!
